name: Fortune Teller
description: Reads cards, stars, palms, or tea leaves — depends on the audience. Some of it's theater. Some of it isn't. The unsettling part is not knowing which.
dialogue_hooks: calling out to you specifically — they've been 'expecting' someone like you
offering a free reading to draw you in
delivering a cryptic warning that turns out to be accurate
asking to read your palm and going pale at what they see
voice_patterns: theatrical mystic — every sentence drips with drama
quiet seer — speaks reluctantly, as if the visions are a burden
carnival barker — all show, preys on hope and fear
matter-of-fact prophet — states the future like a weather report

schedule.morning: setting up tent or stall at market
schedule.evening: busy period — people come after dark for serious questions
schedule.night: genuine divination practice, or counting the day's coin
